main_subtitle: SVG, WEBP, standalone HTML code, text signature, or metadata: choose a badge style to access resources for the 5 labels, download the full kit, or generate ready‑to‑use meta tags
intro_heading: Optimize your integration with our official assets
intro_text: Welcome to the TCP/UP resource platform. Beyond graphic assets, you will find a metadata generator to integrate your labels directly into your HTML code. Whether for a web interface or communication material, you will find a badge suited to your design. HTML versions include a secure link to the relevant label page, providing immediate reassurance to your users.
guide_meta_text: The "#meta"metadata generator automatically creates the appropriate tags for the content you want to label (editorial, code, visual, etc.), ready to be inserted directly into your page’s HTML code.
guide_labels_intro: The "#badges"label generator automatically produces visual badges. To ensure perfect visual consistency across all media, we provide the following 4 integration modes:
guide_labels_svg: SVG format: Ideal for the web and high‑resolution documents. Delivers perfect sharpness at any size with minimal file weight
guide_labels_webp: WEBP format: An ultra‑optimized image format for web performance, offering an excellent balance between quality and loading speed
guide_labels_html: Standalone HTML code: The fastest solution for developers. Copy and paste the code directly into your project. The style is inline and includes a link to the relevant label, meaning no external CSS stylesheet is required for correct display
guide_labels_text: Plain text version: Designed for simplicity and universal accessibility. Ideal for social networks (LinkedIn, X), YouTube descriptions, or environments that do not allow HTML. It declares your label clearly and readably for both humans and algorithms without any image files
color_text: Find the 5 labels (HUC, HCA, HCE, ACE, AIC), each available in 7 color variants to match all interfaces (Standard, High Contrast, Print, Dark Mode, and Transparencies).
kit_intro: This complete ZIP kit is the official toolbox for integrating the TCP/UP declaration. It contains all the graphic resources needed for clear and professional labeling of your content:
kit_item1: 35 label badges: The 5 labels (HUC, HCA, HCE, ACE, AIC), each available in 7 color variants
kit_item2: Multi‑platform formats: Each badge is available in SVG (vector for infinite quality) and WEBP (web‑optimized)
kit_item3: Additional resources: The kit also includes official charter icons and logos for your menus and footers
kit_item4: Integration aid: A code_html directory is provided with a summary file containing all ready‑to‑use integration codes
kit_note: Note: This version 1.0 focuses on graphic resources and code. The official usage guide and the complete graphic charter in PDF format are being finalized and will be integrated in a future update.
meta_intro: Create your meta tags in accordance with the Substance/Form logic and the 5 TCP/UP labels.
meta_step1: Add one or more contents using the Add content button
meta_step2: For each content, choose a content key (editorial, visual, code, etc.) and a TCP/UP label (HUC, HCA, HCE, ACE, AIC)
meta_step3: Metadata is generated in real time using the fixed nomenclature: content-origin (Substance), form-origin (Form), and ai-role
meta_step4: Click Copy to save the code to the clipboard, then insert it between the <head> and </head> tags of your page
meta_hint: Copy the code and insert it between the <head> and </head> tags of your page.
badges_heading: Badge generator (SVG, WEBP, text, and standalone HTML code)
badges_intro: Choose a badge style and instantly obtain the resources for the 5 labels.
